testCases.reason: Manual test - requires UI automation with Playwright
testCases.description: Drag file onto drop zone, verify visual hover indicator appears
testCases.expectedResult: Drop zone shows visual feedback (border highlight, text overlay) when file is dragged over
testCases.notes: Requires Playwright or similar UI automation tool for automated execution

testCases.description: Drop 5 MB file, verify upload completes and file appears in uploads folder
testCases.expectedResult: Upload succeeds with 200 status, file saved in correct directory, file integrity preserved
testCases.actualResult: Successfully uploaded 5 MB file to public/documents/ACTE-2024-001/uploads/ with correct size verification

testCases.description: Drop 20 MB file, verify error message 'File exceeds 15 MB limit'
testCases.expectedResult: Upload fails with 413 status code, clear error message about size limit, no file saved to filesystem
testCases.actualResult: Correctly rejected oversized file (20 MB) with 413 status. Error message: 'File exceeds 15.0 MB size limit. File size: 20.00 MB'

testCases.description: Drop multiple files, verify all valid files upload sequentially
testCases.expectedResult: All 3 files upload successfully, files processed sequentially, all files saved in correct directory
testCases.actualResult: Successfully uploaded 3 files sequentially (1 MB, 3 MB, 5 MB). All files verified in filesystem with correct sizes.
assertions: All files exist at expected paths

testCases.reason: Manual test - requires UI automation with Playwright
testCases.description: Upload in progress, verify progress bar displays percentage
testCases.expectedResult: Progress bar component appears immediately, percentage updates in real-time (0%-100%), visual progress indicator matches percentage
testCases.notes: Requires UI automation with Playwright or manual verification to observe progress bar updates

testCases.reason: Manual test - requires UI automation with Playwright
testCases.description: Upload succeeds, verify success toast notification shown
testCases.expectedResult: Success toast appears after upload completion, toast message includes file name, toast has success styling, toast auto-dismisses gracefully
testCases.notes: Requires UI automation with Playwright or manual verification to observe toast behavior

testCases.description: Network error during upload, verify error toast with retry option
testCases.expectedResult: Connection error is raised when backend unavailable
testCases.actualResult: Correctly raised ConnectionError when attempting to connect to unavailable backend (port 9999)
assertions: ConnectionError raised when backend not running
testCases.notes: Full UI test would verify error toast and retry option in frontend

testCases.description: Test upload behavior when backend times out
testCases.notes: Would require backend mock to simulate slow response for timeout testing

testCases.description: Verify uploaded file appears in case document tree under 'uploads' folder
testCases.expectedResult: File saved in correct uploads folder path, file is accessible in filesystem, uploads folder created if doesn't exist
testCases.actualResult: File successfully uploaded to uploads folder. Verified: folder exists, folder is directory, file exists, file size correct (2 MB)
assertions: Uploads folder exists and is directory
Uploaded file exists at expected path
testCases.notes: UI verification would show file appearing in CaseTreeExplorer under uploads folder

testCases.description: Upload file with special characters in name, verify filename sanitized
testCases.expectedResult: Dangerous characters removed or replaced, path traversal attempts blocked, file saved safely in uploads folder
testCases.actualResult: All malicious filename patterns handled correctly: path traversal (../../../etc/passwd.txt), special characters (test<>:"/\|?*.txt), relative paths (../../sensitive_data.txt). Backend sanitized filenames or rejected uploads.
assertions: Files confined to uploads folder (no escape)

testCases.description: Upload duplicate filename, verify handled (overwrite or rename)
testCases.expectedResult: First upload succeeds, second upload either overwrites or creates renamed file, no data corruption
testCases.actualResult: Both uploads succeeded with 200 status. Backend handles duplicates by overwriting the original file with new content.
testCases.notes: Backend strategy is to overwrite existing files. Alternative strategies could include renaming (file_1.txt) or error rejection.

observations: Backend file upload API is fully functional at POST /api/files/upload
File size limit (15 MB) is correctly enforced with 413 status code
Filenames are properly sanitized to prevent path traversal attacks
Multiple file uploads work sequentially without issues
Duplicate filenames are handled by overwriting (current strategy)
API response uses snake_case (file_path, file_name) not camelCase
Network error handling is robust (ConnectionError raised appropriately)
recommendations: Implement Playwright UI tests for drag-drop interaction (TC-01, 05, 06)
Consider alternative duplicate file handling (rename vs overwrite)
Add upload progress tracking verification in automated tests
Consider adding file type validation (MIME type checking)
Add integration tests for frontend FileDropZone component
